text: A sixty-five years old woman has admitted to our clinic with a history of cough. CT scan revealed intralubar sequestration with a feeding artery coming from the descending aorta.
The surgeon begins the uniportal VATS procedure by dissecting the pulmonary ligament located above the aortic branch. The branch is encircled and secured with two double silk ties.

The surgeon uses an endogenous vascular stapler to divide the arterial branch from the aorta. Following this, the left lower lobectomy is initiated, with the arterial trunk to the lower lobe being dissected, encircled, and divided using a vascular step loop.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to dissect the bronchus to the lower lobe, followed by division with a green reload stapler. An inflation test is performed to ensure the branch to the upper lobe is not included in the stapling.

The surgeon divides the left lower pulmonary vein using a Y-tree load in the J-A stapler. The specimen is then placed in a retrieval bag and removed from the thoracic cavity, followed by standard closure procedures.
Chest tube was removed in the second post-operative day and the patient discharged home in very well condition in the third POD.
